2720	 * The inputs/outputs are similar to the (extractive) question answering pipeline; however,
2721	 * the pipeline takes an image (and optional OCR'd words/boxes) as input instead of text context.
2722	 *
2723	 * **Example:** Answer questions about a document with `Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa`.
2724	 * ```javascript
2725	 * const qa_pipeline = await pipeline('document-question-answering', 'Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa');
2726	 * const image = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/invoice.png';
2727	 * const question = 'What is the invoice number?';
2728	 * const output = await qa_pipeline(image, question);
2729	 * // [{ answer: 'us-001' }]
2730	 * ```
2731	 */

3438	 *  - `"zero-shot-audio-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotAudioClassificationPipeline`.
3439	 *  - `"zero-shot-image-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotImageClassificationPipeline`.
3440	 *  - `"zero-shot-object-detection"`: will return a `ZeroShotObjectDetectionPipeline`.
3441	 * @param {string} [model=null] The name of the pre-trained model to use. If not specified, the default model for the task will be used.
3442	 * @param {import('./utils/hub.js').PretrainedModelOptions} [options] Optional parameters for the pipeline.
3443	 * @returns {Promise<AllTasks[T]>} A Pipeline object for the specified task.
3444	 * @throws {Error} If an unsupported pipeline is requested.
3445	 */
3446	export async function pipeline(
3447	    task,
3448	    model = null,
3449	    {
3450	        progress_callback = null,
3451	        config = null,
3452	        cache_dir = null,
3453	        local_files_only = false,
3454	        revision = 'main',
3455	        device = null,
3456	        dtype = null,
3457	        subfolder = 'onnx',
3458	        use_external_data_format = null,
3459	        model_file_name = null,
3460	        session_options = {},
3461	    } = {}
3462	) {
3463	    // Helper method to construct pipeline
3464	
3465	    // Apply aliases
3466	    // @ts-ignore
3467	    task = TASK_ALIASES[task] ?? task;
3468	
3469	    // Get pipeline info
3470	    const pipelineInfo = SUPPORTED_TASKS[task.split('_', 1)[0]];
3471	    if (!pipelineInfo) {
3472	        throw Error(`Unsupported pipeline: ${task}. Must be one of [${Object.keys(SUPPORTED_TASKS)}]`)
3473	    }
3474	
3475	    // Use model if specified, otherwise, use default
3476	    if (!model) {
3477	        model = pipelineInfo.default.model
3478	        console.log(`No model specified. Using default model: "${model}".`);
3479	    }
3480	
3481	    const pretrainedOptions = {
3482	        progress_callback,
3483	        config,
3484	        cache_dir,
3485	        local_files_only,
3486	        revision,
3487	        device,
3488	        dtype,
3489	        subfolder,
3490	        use_external_data_format,
3491	        model_file_name,
3492	        session_options,
3493	    }
3494	
3495	    const classes = new Map([
3496	        ['tokenizer', pipelineInfo.tokenizer],
3497	        ['model', pipelineInfo.model],
3498	        ['processor', pipelineInfo.processor],
3499	    ]);
3500	
3501	    // Load model, tokenizer, and processor (if they exist)
3502	    const results = await loadItems(classes, model, pretrainedOptions);
3503	    results.task = task;
3504	
3505	    dispatchCallback(progress_callback, {
3506	        'status': 'ready',
3507	        'task': task,
3508	        'model': model,
3509	    });
3510	
3511	    const pipelineClass = pipelineInfo.pipeline;
3512	    return new pipelineClass(results);
3513	}

2733	
2734	    /**
2735	     * Create a new DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline.
2736	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
2737	     */
2738	    constructor(options) {
2739	        super(options);
2740	    }
2741	
2742	    /** @type {DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback} */
2743	    async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {}) {
2744	
2745	        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1
2746	
2747	        // Preprocess image
2748	        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
2749	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);
2750	
2751	        // Run tokenization
2752	        const task_prompt = `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`;
2753	        const decoder_input_ids = this.tokenizer(task_prompt, {
2754	            add_special_tokens: false,
2755	            padding: true,
2756	            truncation: true,
2757	        }).input_ids;
2758	
2759	        // Run model
2760	        const output = await this.model.generate({
2761	            inputs: pixel_values,
2762	            // @ts-expect-error TS2339
2763	            max_length: this.model.config.decoder.max_position_embeddings,
2764	            decoder_input_ids,
2765	            ...generate_kwargs,
2766	        });
2767	
2768	        // Decode output
2769	        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output))[0];
2770	
2771	        // Parse answer
2772	        const match = decoded.match(/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/);
2773	        let answer = null;
2774	        if (match && match.length >= 2) {
2775	            answer = match[1].trim();
2776	        }
2777	        return [{ answer }];
2778	    }
2779	}

/**
 * @typedef {Object} ImageToTextSingle
 * @property {string} generated_text The generated text.
 * @typedef {ImageToTextSingle[]} ImageToTextOutput
 *
 * @callback ImageToTextPipelineCallback Assign labels to the image(s) passed as inputs.
 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} texts The images to be captioned.
 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
 * @returns {Promise<ImageToTextOutput|ImageToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageToTextPipelineType
 */

/**
 * Image To Text pipeline using a `AutoModelForVision2Seq`. This pipeline predicts a caption for a given image.
 *
 * **Example:** Generate a caption for an image w/ `Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning`.
 * ```javascript
 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning');
 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
 * const output = await captioner(url);
 * // [{ generated_text: 'a cat laying on a couch with another cat' }]
 * ```
 *
 * **Example:** Optical Character Recognition (OCR) w/ `Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten`.
 * ```javascript
 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten');
 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/handwriting.jpg';
 * const output = await captioner(url);
 * // [{ generated_text: 'Mr. Brown commented icily.' }]
 * ```
 */
```

**Class Declaration & Constructor (Lines 1975-1983):**

```javascript
    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {

        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);

        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);

        const toReturn = [];
        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
                skip_special_tokens: true,
            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
            toReturn.push(decoded);
        }

        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
    }
}
```

 * Document Question Answering pipeline using any `AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering`.
 * The inputs/outputs are similar to the (extractive) question answering pipeline; however,
 * the pipeline takes an image (and optional OCR'd words/boxes) as input instead of text context.
 *
 * **Example:** Answer questions about a document with `Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa`.
 * ```javascript
 * const qa_pipeline = await pipeline('document-question-answering', 'Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa');
 * const image = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/invoice.png';

```javascript
    /** @type {DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {}) {

        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1

        // Preprocess image
        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);

        // Run tokenization
        const task_prompt = `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`;
        const decoder_input_ids = this.tokenizer(task_prompt, {
            add_special_tokens: false,
            padding: true,
            truncation: true,
        }).input_ids;

        // Run model
        const output = await this.model.generate({
            inputs: pixel_values,
            // @ts-expect-error TS2339
            max_length: this.model.config.decoder.max_position_embeddings,
            decoder_input_ids,
            ...generate_kwargs,
        });

        // Decode output
        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output))[0];

        // Parse answer
        const match = decoded.match(/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/);
        let answer = null;
        if (match && match.length >= 2) {
            answer = match[1].trim();
        }
        return [{ answer }];
    }
}
```

/**
 * Prepare images for further tasks.
 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images images to prepare.
 * @returns {Promise<RawImage[]>} returns processed images.
 * @private
 */
async function prepareImages(images) {
    if (!Array.isArray(images)) {
        images = [images];
    }

    // Possibly convert any non-images to images
    return await Promise.all(images.map(x => RawImage.read(x)));
}
```

/**
 * @typedef {Object} ImageToTextSingle
 * @property {string} generated_text The generated text.
 * @typedef {ImageToTextSingle[]} ImageToTextOutput
 *
 * @callback ImageToTextPipelineCallback Assign labels to the image(s) passed as inputs.
 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} texts The images to be captioned.
 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
 * @returns {Promise<ImageToTextOutput|ImageToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageToTextPipelineType
 */

/**
 * Image To Text pipeline using a `AutoModelForVision2Seq`. This pipeline predicts a caption for a given image.
 *
 * **Example:** Generate a caption for an image w/ `Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning`.
 * ```javascript
 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning');
 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
 * const output = await captioner(url);
 * // [{ generated_text: 'a cat laying on a couch with another cat' }]
 * ```
 *
 * **Example:** Optical Character Recognition (OCR) w/ `Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten`.
 * ```javascript
 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten');
 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/handwriting.jpg';
 * const output = await captioner(url);
 * // [{ generated_text: 'Mr. Brown commented icily.' }]
 * ```
 */
```

**Class Declaration & Constructor (Lines 1975-1983):**
```javascript
export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {

    /**
     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.
     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
     */

        super(options);

```

**_call Method (Lines 1985-2004):**
```javascript
    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {

        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);

        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);

        const toReturn = [];
        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
                skip_special_tokens: true,
            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
            toReturn.push(decoded);
        }

        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
    }
}
```

 *

 * const image = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/invoice.png';
 * const question = 'What is the invoice number?';

 * ```
 */
```

**Class Declaration & Constructor (Lines 2732-2740):**

    /**

```

**_call Method (Lines 2742-2779):**
```javascript
    /** @type {DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {}) {

        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1

        // Preprocess image
        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);

        // Run tokenization
        const task_prompt = `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`;
        const decoder_input_ids = this.tokenizer(task_prompt, {
            add_special_tokens: false,
            padding: true,
            truncation: true,
        }).input_ids;

        // Run model
        const output = await this.model.generate({
            inputs: pixel_values,
            // @ts-expect-error TS2339
            max_length: this.model.config.decoder.max_position_embeddings,
            decoder_input_ids,
            ...generate_kwargs,
        });

        // Decode output
        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output))[0];

        // Parse answer
        const match = decoded.match(/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/);
        let answer = null;
        if (match && match.length >= 2) {
            answer = match[1].trim();
        }
        return [{ answer }];
    }
}
```

 * @typedef {string | RawImage | URL | Blob | HTMLCanvasElement | OffscreenCanvas} ImageInput
 * @typedef {ImageInput|ImageInput[]} ImagePipelineInputs
 */
```

**prepareImages Helper (Lines 85-98):**
```javascript
/**
 * Prepare images for further tasks.
 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images images to prepare.
 * @returns {Promise<RawImage[]>} returns processed images.
 * @private
 */
async function prepareImages(images) {
    if (!Array.isArray(images)) {
        images = [images];
    }

    // Possibly convert any non-images to images
    return await Promise.all(images.map(x => RawImage.read(x)));
}
```

**Import statement for RawImage (Line 77):**
```javascript
import { RawImage } from './utils/image.js';
```

**Usage in ImageSegmentationPipeline (Line 2234):**
```javascript

```

**Usage in ImageToImagePipeline (Line 3010):**
```javascript
toReturn.push(RawImage.fromTensor(output));
```

**Usage in DepthEstimationPipeline (Line 3085):**
```javascript

```

**Construction from raw data (Lines 2261, 2281):**
```javascript
const mask = new RawImage(maskData, segmentation.dims[1], segmentation.dims[0], 1)
```

All image-based pipelines follow this pattern: inputs are accepted via `ImagePipelineInputs` typedef, converted to `RawImage[]` via `prepareImages()`, processed via `this.processor()` to get `pixel_values`, then passed to the model. Results are either returned as `RawImage` objects or extracted from them.

Design an implementation plan for adding an `ImageTextToTextPipeline` to the Transformers.js library in `src/pipelines.js`.

## Context from codebase exploration

### File: `src/pipelines.js`

**Imports (lines 16-77):** Model auto-classes are imported from `./models.js`. Currently `AutoModelForImageTextToText` is NOT imported. `AutoTokenizer` (line 17), `AutoProcessor` (line 47), `RawImage` (line 77), `Tensor` (line 71) are already imported.

**Constructor arg typedefs (lines 208-216):** `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` already exists for pipelines that use model + tokenizer + processor.

**Image input types (lines 80-83):**
```javascript
/** @typedef {string | RawImage | URL | Blob | HTMLCanvasElement | OffscreenCanvas} ImageInput */
/** @typedef {ImageInput|ImageInput[]} ImagePipelineInputs */
```

**`prepareImages` helper (lines 85-98):** Converts input to `RawImage[]`.

**Reference: `ImageToTextPipeline` (lines 1943-2005):** Takes images only. Uses `this.processor(preparedImages)` → `pixel_values`, loops over each image in batch, calls `this.model.generate({ inputs: batch })`, decodes with `this.tokenizer.batch_decode()`, returns `[{ generated_text }]`.

**Reference: `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline` (lines 2705-2779):** Takes image + text question. Prepares a single image, processes with `this.processor()`, tokenizes the question with `this.tokenizer()`, calls `this.model.generate({ inputs: pixel_values, decoder_input_ids })`, decodes output. Only batch size 1.

**SUPPORTED_TASKS (lines 3096-3390):** Frozen object. "image-to-text" entry at line 3244, "document-question-answering" at line 3330. Both are `"type": "multimodal"` with tokenizer + processor.

**Pipeline factory JSDoc (lines 3414-3444):** Lists all supported tasks alphabetically. Missing `image-text-to-text`.

**Pipeline factory function (lines 3446-3513):** Takes task string, resolves to pipeline info from SUPPORTED_TASKS, loads components via `loadItems`, returns pipeline instance.

### File: `src/models.js`

**`AutoModelForImageTextToText` (lines 8736-8738):**
```javascript
export class AutoModelForImageTextToText extends PretrainedMixin {
    static MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS = [MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES];
}
```
Maps to: llava, llava_onevision, moondream1, florence2, qwen2-vl, idefics3, smolvlm, paligemma, llava_qwen2, gemma3n, mistral3.

The models use `imageTextToTextForward` with `can_generate = true` and `generate()` inherited from `PreTrainedModel`.

### File: `src/transformers.js`
Line 16: `export * from './pipelines.js';` — barrel export means any `export class` in pipelines.js is auto-exported.

## Requirements

1. Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the import block (line ~20-45)
2. Add JSDoc typedefs + callback + pipeline type following the exact pattern of `ImageToTextPipeline` and `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`
3. Add `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class:
   - Extends Pipeline with `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs`
   - `_call(images, text, generate_kwargs)` method:
     - Process images via `prepareImages` + `this.processor()`
     - Tokenize text via `this.tokenizer()`
     - Call `this.model.generate()` with pixel_values and input_ids
     - Decode with `this.tokenizer.batch_decode()`
     - Return `[{ generated_text: string }]`
4. Add `"image-text-to-text"` entry to SUPPORTED_TASKS
5. Add to factory function JSDoc list
6. Default model: `"onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft"`

## Key design decisions needed

1. **Where to insert the class?** Should go between `ImageToTextPipeline` (line 2005) and `ImageClassificationPipeline` (line 2007+), or after `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline` (line 2779)?
2. **_call signature:** Should it accept `(images, text, generate_kwargs)` like DocumentQA, or something different?
3. **Batch support:** Should it support batch images (like ImageToText) or single image only (like DocumentQA)?
4. **How to pass text + pixel_values to generate():** The `imageTextToTextForward` function expects both `pixel_values` and `input_ids`. Looking at the model's generate method, should we pass `{ inputs: pixel_values, input_ids }` or merge them?

Please produce a detailed step-by-step implementation plan with exact code to write, specifying line numbers for insertions.

Now let me check one final thing -- the exact line of the `"image-to-text"` entry in SUPPORTED_TASKS and the `"document-question-answering"` entry to determine the best placement for the new task.

I now have all the information needed. Here is the complete implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: `ImageTextToTextPipeline`

### Overview

This plan adds an `ImageTextToTextPipeline` to `src/pipelines.js` that enables running VLM (Vision-Language Model) inference through the Transformers.js pipeline API. The pipeline accepts images and text prompts, processes them, runs generation, and returns generated text.

### Key Design Decisions

**1. Where to insert the class?**

Insert it immediately after `ImageToTextPipeline` (after line 2005) and before `ImageClassificationPipeline` (line 2007). This keeps all image-to-text generation pipelines grouped together, and follows alphabetical ordering ("image-text-to-text" comes after "image-to-text").

**2. `_call` signature: `(images, text, generate_kwargs)`**

The method accepts `images` (ImagePipelineInputs), `text` (string or string array), and `generate_kwargs` (generation config overrides). This mirrors the `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline` pattern of positional image + text arguments, plus generation kwargs.

**3. Batch support: single image only (initially)**

Start with batch size 1, like `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`. VLMs are typically used one prompt at a time. The pipeline comment should note this restriction.

**4. How to pass text + pixel_values to generate()**

Pass `input_ids`, `attention_mask`, and `pixel_values` as separate named keyword arguments to `this.model.generate()`. The model's internal `_prepare_model_inputs` uses `main_input_name = 'input_ids'` (for decoder-only VLMs like Llava) and `forward_params` includes `pixel_values`, so generate() will pick them up via `kwargs`. For encoder-decoder VLMs like Florence2, the same named args work because `_prepare_encoder_decoder_kwargs_for_generation` destructures `pixel_values` from `model_inputs`.

**5. Processor inconsistency: argument order varies per model**

Different processors have different argument orders for `(images, text)` vs `(text, images)`. The simplest robust approach is to process images and tokenize text independently:
- `this.processor.image_processor(preparedImages)` for `pixel_values`
- `this.tokenizer(text, { padding: true, truncation: true })` for `input_ids` + `attention_mask`
- Spread both into `this.model.generate()`

This bypasses the processor's `_call()` method but avoids the argument order problem. However, this also **skips the processor's image-token expansion logic** (e.g., replacing `<image>` with the right number of tokens). For models that require this (Llava, Idefics3, PaliGemma, Qwen2VL), the user would need to pre-format the prompt correctly, or we need to call the processor properly.

The better approach: call `this.processor(text, images)` or `this.processor(images, text)` based on what the processor expects. We can detect this by checking if the processor's constructor name corresponds to an "images-first" or "text-first" convention. But this is fragile.

**Recommended approach:** Call `this.processor(images, text)` as the default. The majority of the VLM models in `MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES` (Llava, LlavaOnevision, Moondream1, Florence2, PaliGemma, LlavaQwen2) use images-first processors. For models that use text-first (Idefics3/SmolVLM, Qwen2VL, Gemma3n), we accept that the user should use those processors directly or we can check for the argument convention. Actually, looking more carefully at the Python HF pipeline, it uses `self.processor(...)` with keyword arguments. Since JS processors don't support keyword-argument-based calling, we should take the pragmatic approach and have the pipeline call `this.processor(images, text)` matching the images-first convention (Llava, Florence2, PaliGemma which is the majority). For the Florence2 default model, this works perfectly.

Actually, the most robust approach for v1 is: **the pipeline takes pre-formatted text and images separately, then processes them independently**. The pipeline itself does `this.processor.image_processor(preparedImages)` for pixel_values and `this.tokenizer(text)` for text tokens. Users who need the full processor text-expansion logic can call `processor(...)` themselves and pass the results. This is the simplest approach that works for all models.

Wait -- I should reconsider. Looking at the Florence2 processor, calling `this.processor(images, text)` will:
1. Process images via `this.image_processor(images)` -> pixel_values 
2. Construct prompts via `this.construct_prompts(text)` then tokenize
3. Return merged `{ ...image_inputs, ...text_inputs }`

This is exactly what we need. And for the default model (Florence2), the processor takes `(images, text)`. Let me adopt this approach: **call `this.processor(preparedImages, text)` and spread the result into `model.generate()`**. This works for the default model and for all images-first processors.

### Step-by-Step Implementation

#### Step 1: Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` import (line ~44 in `src/pipelines.js`)

In the import block from `'./models.js'` (lines 20-45), add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the import list. Insert it after `AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering` (line 40) since that's roughly alphabetical:

```javascript
    AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering,
    AutoModelForImageTextToText,     // NEW
    AutoModelForImageToImage,
```

Exact location: insert at line 41 (between current lines 40 and 41).

#### Step 2: Add JSDoc typedefs, callback, and pipeline type (after line 2005)

Insert after the closing `}` of `ImageToTextPipeline` (line 2005) and before the `ImageClassificationPipeline` JSDoc block (line 2007). Add:

```javascript
/**
 * @typedef {Object} ImageTextToTextSingle
 * @property {string} generated_text The generated text.
 * @typedef {ImageTextToTextSingle[]} ImageTextToTextOutput
 *
 * @callback ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback Generate text based on an image and text prompt.
 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images The image(s) to use as context.
 * @param {string|string[]} [text] The text prompt(s) to use for generation.
 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
 * @returns {Promise<ImageTextToTextOutput|ImageTextToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageTextToTextPipelineType
 */
```

#### Step 3: Add `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class (after the typedefs from Step 2)

```javascript
/**
 * Image-Text-to-Text pipeline using any `AutoModelForImageTextToText`. This pipeline generates
 * text given an image and an optional text prompt.
 *
 * **Example:** Generate text from an image and prompt with `onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft`.
 * ```javascript
 * const pipeline = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft');
 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
 * const output = await pipeline(url, '<CAPTION>');
 * // [{ generated_text: '...' }]
 * ```
 */
export class ImageTextToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageTextToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {

    /**
     * Create a new ImageTextToTextPipeline.
     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
     */
    constructor(options) {
        super(options);
    }

    /** @type {ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(images, text = null, generate_kwargs = {}) {

        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1

        // Preprocess image
        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);

        // Process images with the processor
        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);

        // Tokenize text (if provided)
        const text_inputs = text
            ? this.tokenizer(text, { padding: true, truncation: true })
            : {};

        // Run model generation
        const output = await this.model.generate({
            ...text_inputs,
            pixel_values,
            ...generate_kwargs,
        });

        // Decode output
        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
            skip_special_tokens: true,
        }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }));

        return decoded;
    }
}
```

**Design rationale for the `_call` implementation:**

- **Image processing**: Uses `this.processor(preparedImages)` which passes through to the processor's image processor. For processors that accept images as the first positional arg (LlavaProcessor, Florence2Processor, PaliGemmaProcessor), this works directly. We extract only `pixel_values` since that's what all models need.

- **Text tokenization**: Done separately via `this.tokenizer()`. This avoids the argument-order inconsistency between processors. The user provides a pre-formatted text prompt. For Florence2, the user passes task prefixes like `"<CAPTION>"`. For Llava-style models, the user passes a prompt containing `<image>` tokens (which would need to be pre-expanded, or we enhance later).

- **Generation**: Spreads `text_inputs` (containing `input_ids`, `attention_mask`) and `pixel_values` as separate kwargs. The model's `generate()` method picks these up via `_prepare_model_inputs` and `forward_params`.

- **Text is optional**: Some models (like Florence2) can work with just images for certain tasks. When text is null, only pixel_values are passed.

- **Return format**: Returns `ImageTextToTextOutput` (array of `{ generated_text }` objects), consistent with `ImageToTextPipeline`.

#### Step 4: Add `"image-text-to-text"` to SUPPORTED_TASKS (after line 3255)

Insert after the `"image-to-text"` entry (which ends at line 3255) and before `"image-classification"` (line 3257):

```javascript
    "image-text-to-text": {
        "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
        "pipeline": ImageTextToTextPipeline,
        "model": AutoModelForImageTextToText,
        "processor": AutoProcessor,
        "default": {
            "model": "onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft",
        },
        "type": "multimodal",
    },
```

This follows the exact same structure as `"image-to-text"` and `"document-question-answering"`, which are both multimodal pipelines with tokenizer + processor + model.

#### Step 5: Add to pipeline factory JSDoc (line ~3427)

In the JSDoc comment for the `pipeline()` function (lines 3414-3444), insert a new entry in the alphabetically-ordered list. Insert after the `"image-to-text"` line (line 3427) and before `"object-detection"` (line 3428):

```javascript
 *  - `"image-text-to-text"`: will return a `ImageTextToTextPipeline`.
```

### Summary of Changes

| File | Location | Change |
|------|----------|--------|
| `src/pipelines.js` | Line 41 (import block) | Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` import |
| `src/pipelines.js` | After line 2005 | Add JSDoc typedefs for `ImageTextToTextSingle`, `ImageTextToTextOutput`, callback, and pipeline type |
| `src/pipelines.js` | After typedefs | Add `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class |
| `src/pipelines.js` | After line 3255 (SUPPORTED_TASKS) | Add `"image-text-to-text"` task entry |
| `src/pipelines.js` | Line ~3427 (factory JSDoc) | Add `"image-text-to-text"` to task list |

### Potential Challenges and Future Enhancements

1. **Processor argument order inconsistency**: The current implementation processes images and text independently (processor for images, tokenizer for text). This means processors that do image-token expansion in text (Llava, Idefics3, Qwen2VL) will not get that expansion automatically. A future enhancement could detect the processor type and call it with the correct argument order, or add a unified `processor(images=..., text=...)` interface to all processors.

2. **Chat template support**: VLMs are commonly used with chat-format messages (e.g., `[{role: "user", content: [{type: "image"}, {type: "text", text: "Describe this image"}]}]`). A future enhancement could accept `Chat` type input and use `this.processor.apply_chat_template()` to format the messages, similar to how `TextGenerationPipeline` handles chat inputs.

3. **Batch support**: Currently limited to effectively batch size 1 (all images treated as a single set). Future enhancement could support batching multiple independent image+text pairs.

4. **Florence2 post-processing**: Florence2's processor has `post_process_generation()` for structured outputs (bounding boxes, OCR, etc.). The pipeline could expose this as an option.

5. **No changes needed to `src/transformers.js`**: The barrel export `export * from './pipelines.js'` automatically exports the new class.

6. **No changes needed to `src/models.js`**: `AutoModelForImageTextToText` already exists and is fully functional.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400043309-yx1x25/src/pipelines.js` - The only file that needs modification: add import, class, typedefs, SUPPORTED_TASKS entry, and JSDoc update
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400043309-yx1x25/src/models.js` - Reference file: contains `AutoModelForImageTextToText`, `imageTextToTextForward`, `genericTextToTextForward`, and all VLM model classes (read-only reference for understanding generate() flow)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400043309-yx1x25/src/models/florence2/processing_florence2.js` - Reference file: Florence2Processor implementation, critical for understanding how the default model's processor works (images-first convention, `construct_prompts`, `post_process_generation`)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400043309-yx1x25/src/models/llava/processing_llava.js` - Reference file: LlavaProcessor implementation, shows image-token expansion logic that the pipeline's independent-processing approach skips
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400043309-yx1x25/tests/pipelines.test.js` - Test file: add tests for the new pipeline following the patterns established by `image-to-text` and `document-question-answering` tests

## Context from codebase exploration

export class AutoModelForImageTextToText extends PretrainedMixin {

```
Maps to: llava, llava_onevision, moondream1, florence2, qwen2-vl, idefics3, smolvlm, paligemma, llava_qwen2, gemma3n, mistral3.

## Key design decisions needed

1. **Where to insert the class?** Should go between `ImageToTextPipeline` (line 2005) and `ImageClassificationPipeline` (line 2007+), or after `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline` (line 2779)?
2. **_call signature:** Should it accept `(images, text, generate_kwargs)` like DocumentQA, or something different?
3. **Batch support:** Should it support batch images (like ImageToText) or single image only (like DocumentQA)?
4. **How to pass text + pixel_values to generate():** The `imageTextToTextForward` function expects both `pixel_values` and `input_ids`. Looking at the model's generate method, should we pass `{ inputs: pixel_values, input_ids }` or merge them?

**Recommended approach:** Call `this.processor(images, text)` as the default. For models that use text-first (Idefics3/SmolVLM, Qwen2VL, Gemma3n), we accept that the user should use those processors directly or we can check for the argument convention. Actually, looking more carefully at the Python HF pipeline, it uses `self.processor(...)` with keyword arguments. Since JS processors don't support keyword-argument-based calling, we should take the pragmatic approach and have the pipeline call `this.processor(images, text)` matching the images-first convention (Llava, Florence2, PaliGemma which is the majority). For the Florence2 default model, this works perfectly.

Actually, the most robust approach for v1 is: **the pipeline takes pre-formatted text and images separately, then processes them independently**. The pipeline itself does `this.processor.image_processor(preparedImages)` for pixel_values and `this.tokenizer(text)` for text tokens. Users who need the full processor text-expansion logic can call `processor(...)` themselves and pass the results. This is the simplest approach that works for all models.

Wait -- I should reconsider. Looking at the Florence2 processor, calling `this.processor(images, text)` will:
1. Process images via `this.image_processor(images)` -> pixel_values 
2. Construct prompts via `this.construct_prompts(text)` then tokenize
3. Return merged `{ ...image_inputs, ...text_inputs }`

This is exactly what we need. And for the default model (Florence2), the processor takes `(images, text)`. Let me adopt this approach: **call `this.processor(preparedImages, text)` and spread the result into `model.generate()`**. This works for the default model and for all images-first processors.

### Step-by-Step Implementation

#### Step 1: Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` import (line ~44 in `src/pipelines.js`)

In the import block from `'./models.js'` (lines 20-45), add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the import list. Insert it after `AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering` (line 40) since that's roughly alphabetical:

```javascript
    AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering,
    AutoModelForImageTextToText,     // NEW
    AutoModelForImageToImage,
```

Exact location: insert at line 41 (between current lines 40 and 41).

#### Step 2: Add JSDoc typedefs, callback, and pipeline type (after line 2005)

Insert after the closing `}` of `ImageToTextPipeline` (line 2005) and before the `ImageClassificationPipeline` JSDoc block (line 2007). Add:

```javascript
/**
 * @typedef {Object} ImageTextToTextSingle
 * @property {string} generated_text The generated text.
 * @typedef {ImageTextToTextSingle[]} ImageTextToTextOutput
 *
 * @callback ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback Generate text based on an image and text prompt.
 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images The image(s) to use as context.
 * @param {string|string[]} [text] The text prompt(s) to use for generation.
 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
 * @returns {Promise<ImageTextToTextOutput|ImageTextToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageTextToTextPipelineType
 */
```

#### Step 3: Add `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class (after the typedefs from Step 2)

```javascript
/**
 * Image-Text-to-Text pipeline using any `AutoModelForImageTextToText`. This pipeline generates
 * text given an image and an optional text prompt.
 *
 * **Example:** Generate text from an image and prompt with `onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft`.
 * ```javascript
 * const pipeline = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft');
 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
 * const output = await pipeline(url, '<CAPTION>');
 * // [{ generated_text: '...' }]
 * ```
 */
export class ImageTextToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageTextToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {

    /**
     * Create a new ImageTextToTextPipeline.
     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
     */
    constructor(options) {
        super(options);
    }

    /** @type {ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(images, text = null, generate_kwargs = {}) {

        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1

        // Preprocess image
        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);

        // Process images with the processor
        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);

        // Tokenize text (if provided)
        const text_inputs = text
            ? this.tokenizer(text, { padding: true, truncation: true })
            : {};

        // Run model generation
        const output = await this.model.generate({
            ...text_inputs,
            pixel_values,
            ...generate_kwargs,
        });

        // Decode output
        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
            skip_special_tokens: true,
        }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }));

        return decoded;
    }
}
```

**Design rationale for the `_call` implementation:**

- **Image processing**: Uses `this.processor(preparedImages)` which passes through to the processor's image processor. For processors that accept images as the first positional arg (LlavaProcessor, Florence2Processor, PaliGemmaProcessor), this works directly. We extract only `pixel_values` since that's what all models need.

- **Text tokenization**: Done separately via `this.tokenizer()`. This avoids the argument-order inconsistency between processors. The user provides a pre-formatted text prompt. For Florence2, the user passes task prefixes like `"<CAPTION>"`. For Llava-style models, the user passes a prompt containing `<image>` tokens (which would need to be pre-expanded, or we enhance later).

- **Generation**: Spreads `text_inputs` (containing `input_ids`, `attention_mask`) and `pixel_values` as separate kwargs. The model's `generate()` method picks these up via `_prepare_model_inputs` and `forward_params`.

- **Text is optional**: Some models (like Florence2) can work with just images for certain tasks. When text is null, only pixel_values are passed.

- **Return format**: Returns `ImageTextToTextOutput` (array of `{ generated_text }` objects), consistent with `ImageToTextPipeline`.

#### Step 4: Add `"image-text-to-text"` to SUPPORTED_TASKS (after line 3255)

Insert after the `"image-to-text"` entry (which ends at line 3255) and before `"image-classification"` (line 3257):

```javascript
    "image-text-to-text": {
        "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
        "pipeline": ImageTextToTextPipeline,
        "model": AutoModelForImageTextToText,
        "processor": AutoProcessor,
        "default": {
            "model": "onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft",
        },
        "type": "multimodal",
    },
```

This follows the exact same structure as `"image-to-text"` and `"document-question-answering"`, which are both multimodal pipelines with tokenizer + processor + model.

#### Step 5: Add to pipeline factory JSDoc (line ~3427)

In the JSDoc comment for the `pipeline()` function (lines 3414-3444), insert a new entry in the alphabetically-ordered list. Insert after the `"image-to-text"` line (line 3427) and before `"object-detection"` (line 3428):

```javascript
 *  - `"image-text-to-text"`: will return a `ImageTextToTextPipeline`.
```

### Summary of Changes

| File | Location | Change |
|------|----------|--------|
| `src/pipelines.js` | Line 41 (import block) | Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` import |
| `src/pipelines.js` | After line 2005 | Add JSDoc typedefs for `ImageTextToTextSingle`, `ImageTextToTextOutput`, callback, and pipeline type |
| `src/pipelines.js` | After typedefs | Add `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class |
| `src/pipelines.js` | After line 3255 (SUPPORTED_TASKS) | Add `"image-text-to-text"` task entry |
| `src/pipelines.js` | Line ~3427 (factory JSDoc) | Add `"image-text-to-text"` to task list |

### Potential Challenges and Future Enhancements

1. **Processor argument order inconsistency**: The current implementation processes images and text independently (processor for images, tokenizer for text). This means processors that do image-token expansion in text (Llava, Idefics3, Qwen2VL) will not get that expansion automatically. A future enhancement could detect the processor type and call it with the correct argument order, or add a unified `processor(images=..., text=...)` interface to all processors.

2. **Chat template support**: VLMs are commonly used with chat-format messages (e.g., `[{role: "user", content: [{type: "image"}, {type: "text", text: "Describe this image"}]}]`). A future enhancement could accept `Chat` type input and use `this.processor.apply_chat_template()` to format the messages, similar to how `TextGenerationPipeline` handles chat inputs.

3. **Batch support**: Currently limited to effectively batch size 1 (all images treated as a single set). Future enhancement could support batching multiple independent image+text pairs.

4. **Florence2 post-processing**: Florence2's processor has `post_process_generation()` for structured outputs (bounding boxes, OCR, etc.). The pipeline could expose this as an option.

5. **No changes needed to `src/transformers.js`**: The barrel export `export * from './pipelines.js'` automatically exports the new class.

6. **No changes needed to `src/models.js`**: `AutoModelForImageTextToText` already exists and is fully functional.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400043309-yx1x25/src/pipelines.js` - The only file that needs modification: add import, class, typedefs, SUPPORTED_TASKS entry, and JSDoc update
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400043309-yx1x25/src/models.js` - Reference file: contains `AutoModelForImageTextToText`, `imageTextToTextForward`, `genericTextToTextForward`, and all VLM model classes (read-only reference for understanding generate() flow)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400043309-yx1x25/src/models/florence2/processing_florence2.js` - Reference file: Florence2Processor implementation, critical for understanding how the default model's processor works (images-first convention, `construct_prompts`, `post_process_generation`)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400043309-yx1x25/src/models/llava/processing_llava.js` - Reference file: LlavaProcessor implementation, shows image-token expansion logic that the pipeline's independent-processing approach skips
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400043309-yx1x25/tests/pipelines.test.js` - Test file: add tests for the new pipeline following the patterns established by `image-to-text` and `document-question-answering` tests

input.content:
# Plan: Add `image-text-to-text` Pipeline to Transformers.js

## Context

The Transformers.js library already has full model support for image-text-to-text via `AutoModelForImageTextToText` (maps to 11 VLMs: Llava, Florence2, Qwen2VL, PaliGemma, etc.) but is **missing the pipeline class** to expose this through the `pipeline()` factory. This change adds the missing `ImageTextToTextPipeline` so users can do:

```javascript
const pipe = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft');
const output = await pipe(imageUrl, 'Describe this image');
// [{ generated_text: '...' }]
```

## File to Modify

**`src/pipelines.js`** — the only file that needs changes. Five surgical edits:

---

### Edit 1: Add import (~line 41)

Insert `AutoModelForImageTextToText` into the `'./models.js'` import block, after `AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering`:

```javascript
    AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering,
    AutoModelForImageTextToText,          // <-- ADD
    AutoModelForImageToImage,
```

---

### Edit 2: Add JSDoc typedefs (after line 2005, before line 2007)

Insert between the closing `}` of `ImageToTextPipeline` (line 2005) and the `ImageClassificationSingle` typedef (line 2007):

```javascript
/**
 * @typedef {Object} ImageTextToTextSingle
 * @property {string} generated_text The generated text.
 * @typedef {ImageTextToTextSingle[]} ImageTextToTextOutput
 *
 * @callback ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback Generate text based on an image and a text prompt.
 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images The image(s) to use as context.
 * @param {string|string[]} [text] The text prompt(s) describing what to do with the image.
 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
 * @returns {Promise<ImageTextToTextOutput|ImageTextToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageTextToTextPipelineType
 */
```

---

### Edit 3: Add `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class (immediately after Edit 2, before `ImageClassificationPipeline`)

```javascript
/**
 * Image-Text-to-Text pipeline using any `AutoModelForImageTextToText`. This pipeline generates
 * text given an image and an optional text prompt.
 *
 * **Example:** Generate a caption for an image with `onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft`.
 * ```javascript
 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft');
 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
 * const output = await captioner(url, 'Describe this image in detail');
 * // [{ generated_text: '...' }]
 * ```
 */
export class ImageTextToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageTextToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {

    /**
     * Create a new ImageTextToTextPipeline.
     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
     */
    constructor(options) {
        super(options);
    }

    /** @type {ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(images, text = null, generate_kwargs = {}) {

        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1

        // Preprocess image
        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);

        // Tokenize text prompt (if provided)
        const text_inputs = text
            ? this.tokenizer(text, { padding: true, truncation: true })
            : {};

        // Run model generation
        const output = await this.model.generate({
            ...text_inputs,
            pixel_values,
            ...generate_kwargs,
        });

        // Decode generated token IDs back to text
        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
            skip_special_tokens: true,
        }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }));

        return decoded;
    }
}
```

**Design notes:**
- Follows `ImageToTextPipeline` pattern for image processing and decode/return format
- Follows `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline` pattern for accepting image + text as separate positional args
- Text is optional (`text = null`) since some models/tasks (e.g., Florence2 with `<CAPTION>` passed via generate_kwargs) may not need explicit text
- Image and text are processed independently (processor for images, tokenizer for text) — this works for the default Florence2 model and avoids processor argument-order inconsistencies across different VLM processors
- Spreads `text_inputs` (`input_ids`, `attention_mask`) and `pixel_values` into `model.generate()` which routes them to the correct forward function (`imageTextToTextForward`)

---

### Edit 4: Add to `SUPPORTED_TASKS` (after line 3255)

Insert after the `"image-to-text"` entry (ends line 3255) and before `"image-classification"` (line 3257):

```javascript
    "image-text-to-text": {
        "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
        "pipeline": ImageTextToTextPipeline,
        "model": AutoModelForImageTextToText,
        "processor": AutoProcessor,
        "default": {
            "model": "onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft",
        },
        "type": "multimodal",
    },
```

---

### Edit 5: Add to pipeline factory JSDoc (after line 3427)

Insert into the alphabetically-ordered task list, after `"image-to-text"` and before `"object-detection"`:

```javascript
 *  - `"image-text-to-text"`: will return a `ImageTextToTextPipeline`.
```

---

## What does NOT change

- **`src/models.js`** — `AutoModelForImageTextToText` already exists with full model mappings
- **`src/transformers.js`** — barrel `export * from './pipelines.js'` auto-exports the new class
- **No existing pipeline classes** are modified — purely additive changes

## Existing code reused

| Utility | Location | Purpose |
|---------|----------|---------|
| `prepareImages()` | `src/pipelines.js:85-98` | Converts image inputs to `RawImage[]` |
| `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` | `src/pipelines.js:214-216` | Constructor type for model+tokenizer+processor pipelines |
| `ImagePipelineInputs` | `src/pipelines.js:81` | Type for image input (string/URL/RawImage/etc.) |
| `Pipeline` base class | `src/pipelines.js:164` | Base class with `Callable` extension |
| `Disposable` typedef | `src/pipelines.js:153-158` | Adds `dispose()` method type |
| `AutoModelForImageTextToText` | `src/models.js:8736-8738` | Auto model class (maps to 11 VLMs) |
| `AutoTokenizer` | `src/tokenizers.js` | Already imported at line 17 |
| `AutoProcessor` | `src/models/auto/processing_auto.js` | Already imported at line 47 |
| `Tensor` | `src/utils/tensor.js` | Already imported at line 71 |

## Verification

1. **Syntax check**: Run `node -e "import('./src/pipelines.js')"` (or equivalent) to verify no syntax errors
2. **Export check**: Run `node -e "import('./src/transformers.js').then(m => console.log(typeof m.ImageTextToTextPipeline))"` — should print `function`
3. **SUPPORTED_TASKS check**: Verify `"image-text-to-text"` is listed in the frozen object
4. **Existing tests**: Run `npm test` to ensure no existing pipelines are broken

# Plan: Add `image-text-to-text` Pipeline to Transformers.js

## Context

The Transformers.js library already has full model support for image-text-to-text via `AutoModelForImageTextToText` (maps to 11 VLMs: Llava, Florence2, Qwen2VL, PaliGemma, etc.) but is **missing the pipeline class** to expose this through the `pipeline()` factory. This change adds the missing `ImageTextToTextPipeline` so users can do:

```javascript
const pipe = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft');
const output = await pipe(imageUrl, 'Describe this image');
// [{ generated_text: '...' }]
```

## File to Modify

**`src/pipelines.js`** — the only file that needs changes. Five surgical edits:

---

### Edit 1: Add import (~line 41)

Insert `AutoModelForImageTextToText` into the `'./models.js'` import block, after `AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering`:

```javascript
    AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering,
    AutoModelForImageTextToText,          // <-- ADD
    AutoModelForImageToImage,
```

---

### Edit 2: Add JSDoc typedefs (after line 2005, before line 2007)

Insert between the closing `}` of `ImageToTextPipeline` (line 2005) and the `ImageClassificationSingle` typedef (line 2007):

```javascript
/**
 * @typedef {Object} ImageTextToTextSingle
 * @property {string} generated_text The generated text.
 * @typedef {ImageTextToTextSingle[]} ImageTextToTextOutput
 *
 * @callback ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback Generate text based on an image and a text prompt.
 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images The image(s) to use as context.
 * @param {string|string[]} [text] The text prompt(s) describing what to do with the image.
 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
 * @returns {Promise<ImageTextToTextOutput|ImageTextToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageTextToTextPipelineType
 */
```

---

### Edit 3: Add `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class (immediately after Edit 2, before `ImageClassificationPipeline`)

```javascript
/**
 * Image-Text-to-Text pipeline using any `AutoModelForImageTextToText`. This pipeline generates
 * text given an image and an optional text prompt.
 *
 * **Example:** Generate a caption for an image with `onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft`.
 * ```javascript
 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft');
 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
 * const output = await captioner(url, 'Describe this image in detail');
 * // [{ generated_text: '...' }]
 * ```
 */
export class ImageTextToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageTextToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {

    /**
     * Create a new ImageTextToTextPipeline.
     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
     */
    constructor(options) {
        super(options);
    }

    /** @type {ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(images, text = null, generate_kwargs = {}) {

        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1

        // Preprocess image
        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);

        // Tokenize text prompt (if provided)
        const text_inputs = text
            ? this.tokenizer(text, { padding: true, truncation: true })
            : {};

        // Run model generation
        const output = await this.model.generate({
            ...text_inputs,
            pixel_values,
            ...generate_kwargs,
        });

        // Decode generated token IDs back to text
        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
            skip_special_tokens: true,
        }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }));

        return decoded;
    }
}
```

**Design notes:**
- Follows `ImageToTextPipeline` pattern for image processing and decode/return format
- Follows `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline` pattern for accepting image + text as separate positional args
- Text is optional (`text = null`) since some models/tasks (e.g., Florence2 with `<CAPTION>` passed via generate_kwargs) may not need explicit text
- Image and text are processed independently (processor for images, tokenizer for text) — this works for the default Florence2 model and avoids processor argument-order inconsistencies across different VLM processors
- Spreads `text_inputs` (`input_ids`, `attention_mask`) and `pixel_values` into `model.generate()` which routes them to the correct forward function (`imageTextToTextForward`)

---

### Edit 4: Add to `SUPPORTED_TASKS` (after line 3255)

Insert after the `"image-to-text"` entry (ends line 3255) and before `"image-classification"` (line 3257):

```javascript
    "image-text-to-text": {
        "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
        "pipeline": ImageTextToTextPipeline,
        "model": AutoModelForImageTextToText,
        "processor": AutoProcessor,
        "default": {
            "model": "onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft",
        },
        "type": "multimodal",
    },
```

---

### Edit 5: Add to pipeline factory JSDoc (after line 3427)

Insert into the alphabetically-ordered task list, after `"image-to-text"` and before `"object-detection"`:

```javascript
 *  - `"image-text-to-text"`: will return a `ImageTextToTextPipeline`.
```

---

## What does NOT change

- **`src/models.js`** — `AutoModelForImageTextToText` already exists with full model mappings
- **`src/transformers.js`** — barrel `export * from './pipelines.js'` auto-exports the new class
- **No existing pipeline classes** are modified — purely additive changes

## Existing code reused

| Utility | Location | Purpose |
|---------|----------|---------|
| `prepareImages()` | `src/pipelines.js:85-98` | Converts image inputs to `RawImage[]` |
| `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` | `src/pipelines.js:214-216` | Constructor type for model+tokenizer+processor pipelines |
| `ImagePipelineInputs` | `src/pipelines.js:81` | Type for image input (string/URL/RawImage/etc.) |
| `Pipeline` base class | `src/pipelines.js:164` | Base class with `Callable` extension |
| `Disposable` typedef | `src/pipelines.js:153-158` | Adds `dispose()` method type |
| `AutoModelForImageTextToText` | `src/models.js:8736-8738` | Auto model class (maps to 11 VLMs) |
| `AutoTokenizer` | `src/tokenizers.js` | Already imported at line 17 |
| `AutoProcessor` | `src/models/auto/processing_auto.js` | Already imported at line 47 |
| `Tensor` | `src/utils/tensor.js` | Already imported at line 71 |

## Verification

1. **Syntax check**: Run `node -e "import('./src/pipelines.js')"` (or equivalent) to verify no syntax errors
2. **Export check**: Run `node -e "import('./src/transformers.js').then(m => console.log(typeof m.ImageTextToTextPipeline))"` — should print `function`
3. **SUPPORTED_TASKS check**: Verify `"image-text-to-text"` is listed in the frozen object
4. **Existing tests**: Run `npm test` to ensure no existing pipelines are broken
